name: fuo-ytmusic
documentation:
# YouTube Music plugin for FeelUOwn player

## Prerequisites

Install [FeelUOwn](https://github.com/feeluown/FeelUOwn) before installing this plugin.
Sees: [Documentation](https://feeluown.readthedocs.io/)

## Installation

```shell
pip install fuo-ytmusic  # Lastest stable release
pip install https://github.com/feeluown/feeluown-ytmusic.git  # master branch
poetry install  # Local development
```

## Configuration

### Proxies

```python
# In ~/.fuorc
config.deffield('YTM_HTTP_PROXY', type_=str, default='', desc='YouTube Music 代理设置')
config.YTM_HTTP_PROXY='127.0.0.1:10809'
```

## Contributing

Pull requests are welcome. For major changes, please create a new issue first to discuss what you'd like to change.

Please make sure to compatible with [FeelUOwn](https://github.com/feeluown/FeelUOwn) lastest stable release.

## License

This project is licensed under the [GPLv3](LICENSE.txt).

modules.name: fuo_ytmusic.timeparse
documentation:
timeparse.py
(c) Will Roberts <wildwilhelm@gmail.com>  1 February, 2014

Implements a single function, `timeparse`, which can parse various
kinds of time expressions.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.timeparse._interpret_as_minutes
documentation:
Times like "1:22" are ambiguous; do they represent minutes and seconds
or hours and minutes? By default, timeparse assumes the latter. Call
this function after parsing out a dictionary to change that assumption.

```pycon
>>> import pprint
>>> pprint.pprint(_interpret_as_minutes('1:24', {'secs': '24', 'mins': '1'}))
{'hours': '1', 'mins': '24'}
```

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.timeparse.timeparse
documentation:
Parse a time expression, returning it as a number of seconds. If
possible, the return value will be an `int`; if this is not
possible, the return will be a `float`. Returns `None` if a time
expression cannot be parsed from the given string.

Arguments:
\- `sval`: the string value to parse

```pycon
>>> timeparse('1:24')
84
>>> timeparse(':22')
22
>>> timeparse('1 minute, 24 secs')
84
>>> timeparse('1m24s')
84
>>> timeparse('1.2 minutes')
72
>>> timeparse('1.2 seconds')
1.2
```

Time expressions can be signed.

```pycon
>>> timeparse('- 1 minute')
-60
>>> timeparse('+ 1 minute')
60
```

If granularity is specified as `minutes`, then ambiguous digits following
a colon will be interpreted as minutes; otherwise they are considered seconds.

```pycon
>>> timeparse('1:30')
90
>>> timeparse('1:30', granularity='minutes')
5400
```

modules.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher
documentation:
:::{note}
This file is copied from pytube and we do some changes on it.
Every changes is noted by a comment with prefix updated: .
This script should frequently be updated to adapt to the changes
in the YouTube website and pytube does not.
:::

This module contains all logic necessary to decipher the signature.

YouTube's strategy to restrict downloading videos is to send a ciphered version
of the signature to the client, along with the decryption algorithm obfuscated
in JavaScript. For the clients to play the videos, JavaScript must take the
ciphered version, cycle it through a series of "transform functions," and then
signs the media URL with the output.

This module is responsible for (1) finding and extracting those "transform
functions" (2) maps them to Python equivalents and (3) taking the ciphered
signature and decoding it.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.get_transform_plan
documentation:
Extract the "transform plan".

The "transform plan" is the functions that the ciphered signature is
cycled through to obtain the actual signature.

:param str js:
    The contents of the base.js asset file.

**Example**:

['DE.AJ(a,15)',
'DE.VR(a,3)',
'DE.AJ(a,51)',
'DE.VR(a,3)',
'DE.kT(a,51)',
'DE.kT(a,8)',
'DE.VR(a,3)',
'DE.kT(a,21)']

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.get_transform_object
documentation:
Extract the "transform object".

The "transform object" contains the function definitions referenced in the
"transform plan". The `var` argument is the obfuscated variable name
which contains these functions, for example, given the function call
`DE.AJ(a,15)` returned by the transform plan, "DE" would be the var.

```
param str js

The contents of the base.js asset file.

param str var

The obfuscated variable name that stores an object with all functions
that descrambles the signature.
```

**Example**:

```pycon
>>> get_transform_object(js, 'DE')
['AJ:function(a){a.reverse()}',
'VR:function(a,b){a.splice(0,b)}',
'kT:function(a,b){var c=a[0];a[0]=a[b%a.length];a[b]=c}']
```

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.get_transform_map
documentation:
Build a transform function lookup.

Build a lookup table of obfuscated JavaScript function names to the
Python equivalents.

:param str js:
    The contents of the base.js asset file.
:param str var:
    The obfuscated variable name that stores an object with all functions
    that descrambles the signature.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.get_throttling_function_array
documentation:
Extract the "c" array.

:param str js:
    The contents of the base.js asset file.
:returns:
    The array of various integers, arrays, and functions.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.get_throttling_plan
documentation:
Extract the "throttling plan".

The "throttling plan" is a list of tuples used for calling functions
in the c array. The first element of the tuple is the index of the
function to call, and any remaining elements of the tuple are arguments
to pass to that function.

:param str js:
    The contents of the base.js asset file.
:returns:
    The full function code for computing the throttlign parameter.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.reverse
documentation:
Reverse elements in a list.

This function is equivalent to:

```javascript
function(a, b) { a.reverse() }
```

This method takes an unused `b` variable as their transform functions
universally sent two arguments.

**Example**:

```pycon
>>> reverse([1, 2, 3, 4])
[4, 3, 2, 1]
```

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.splice
documentation:
Add/remove items to/from a list.

This function is equivalent to:

```javascript
function(a, b) { a.splice(0, b) }
```

**Example**:

```pycon
>>> splice([1, 2, 3, 4], 2)
[1, 2]
```

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.swap
documentation:
Swap positions at b modulus the list length.

This function is equivalent to:

```javascript
function(a, b) { var c=a[0];a[0]=a[b%a.length];a[b]=c }
```

**Example**:

```pycon
>>> swap([1, 2, 3, 4], 2)
[3, 2, 1, 4]
```

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_reverse
documentation:
Reverses the input list.

Needs to do an in-place reversal so that the passed list gets changed.
To accomplish this, we create a reversed copy, and then change each
indvidual element.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_push
documentation: Pushes an element onto a list.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_mod_func
documentation:
Perform the modular function from the throttling array functions.

In the javascript, the modular operation is as follows:
e = (e % d.length + d.length) % d.length

We simply translate this to python here.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_unshift
fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_cipher_function
fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_nested_splice
documentation:
Nested splice function in throttling js.

In the javascript, the operation is as follows:
function(d,e){
    e=(e%d.length+d.length)%d.length;
    d.splice(
        0,
        1,
        d.splice(
            e,
            1,
            d[0]
        )[0]
    )
}

While testing, all this seemed to do is swap element 0 and e,
but the actual process is preserved in case there was an edge
case that was not considered.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_prepend
fuo_ytmusic.cipher.throttling_swap
documentation: Swap positions of the 0'th and e'th elements in-place.

functions.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.js_splice
documentation:
Implementation of javascript's splice function.

```
param list arr

Array to splice

param int start

Index at which to start changing the array

param int delete_count

Number of elements to delete from the array

param *items

source:9: (WARNING/2) Inline emphasis start-string without end-string.

Items to add to the array
```

Reference: <https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/splice> # noqa:E501

methods.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.Cipher.get_signature
documentation:
Decipher the signature.

Taking the ciphered signature, applies the transform functions.

```
param str ciphered_signature

The ciphered signature sent in the player_config.

rtype

str

returns

Decrypted signature required to download the media content.
```

methods.name: fuo_ytmusic.cipher.Cipher.parse_function
documentation:
Parse the Javascript transform function.

Break a JavaScript transform function down into a two element `tuple`
containing the function name and some integer-based argument.

```
param str js_func

The JavaScript version of the transform function.

rtype

tuple

returns

two element tuple containing the function name and an argument.
```

**Example**:

parse_function('DE.AJ(a,15)')
('AJ', 15)
